caseStudies.title: Intelligent Document Processing
caseStudies.description: Automating extraction from unstructured legal and financial documents.
caseStudies.overview: A legal services firm processed thousands of contracts, filings, and correspondence monthly, with teams of paralegals manually extracting key clauses, dates, and obligations. The goal was to automate extraction to allow legal professionals to focus on higher-value advisory work.
caseStudies.problem: Manual document review was the primary bottleneck in the firm's workflow, with each contract requiring 2–4 hours of paralegal time. Error rates in extraction were estimated at 3–5%, creating downstream risks in contract management. High paralegal turnover added ongoing training costs.
caseStudies.solution: A document intelligence pipeline was built using large language models fine-tuned for legal document understanding. Extracted entities and clauses were structured into a review interface that presented results alongside the source document for human verification. A confidence scoring system flagged low-certainty extractions for priority review.

results.summary: Paralegals now review AI-extracted results rather than performing manual extraction, shifting their work to verification and exception handling. The firm has taken on 40% more client volume without adding headcount.

caseStudies.title: Conversational Customer Support
caseStudies.description: AI-first support deflecting 70% of tier-1 tickets.
caseStudies.overview: A SaaS company's customer support team was overwhelmed by repetitive tier-1 queries that represented 70% of ticket volume but required relatively little expertise to resolve. The goal was to deploy an AI support assistant that could handle these queries end-to-end.
caseStudies.problem: Support agents spent the majority of their time on routine queries such as password resets, billing questions, and feature how-tos, leaving complex technical issues under-resourced. Average response time was 8 hours, causing customer frustration and churn.
caseStudies.solution: A conversational AI assistant was trained on the product knowledge base, historical support tickets, and documentation. The assistant handled tier-1 queries end-to-end, escalating to human agents with a structured summary when it encountered queries outside its confidence threshold. Agent-assist features provided real-time suggestions during human-handled conversations.

results.summary: Human agents now focus exclusively on complex technical issues, improving resolution quality and job satisfaction. The AI assistant handles the majority of support volume at a fraction of the cost of human agents.

caseStudies.title: Code Review Automation
caseStudies.description: Embedding AI review into the engineering workflow at scale.
caseStudies.overview: A large engineering organisation was experiencing code review bottlenecks, with pull requests waiting an average of 3 days for a human reviewer. Junior engineers received inconsistent feedback depending on which senior engineer reviewed their work. The goal was to augment human review with AI-powered analysis.
caseStudies.problem: Senior engineers spent 20–30% of their time on code review, creating a bottleneck for the entire organisation. Code quality was inconsistent across teams, and security vulnerabilities were occasionally merged due to reviewer fatigue. New engineers received varying levels of mentorship through review depending on team culture.
caseStudies.solution: An AI code review system was integrated into the pull request workflow, automatically analysing changes for correctness, security issues, performance implications, and style consistency. The system posted structured comments with explanations and suggested fixes. Human reviewers were presented with an AI summary to accelerate their own review.

stats.label: Avg. first feedback time (down from 3 days)

results.summary: Engineering throughput has increased substantially without reducing code quality. Junior engineers receive consistent, high-quality feedback on every pull request, accelerating their development and improving team-wide code standards.
